stories.short_label: Bern Election: City vs. Countryside Divide
stories.summary: Comprehensive investigative report on political and social polarization between urban Bern and rural areas during cantonal elections. Features SVP politician Daniel Bichsel campaigning in rural areas with conservative messaging about taxes and bureaucracy, and Green politician Aline Trede campaigning in the countryside with environmental and social policy focus. Includes analysis by political geographer Michael Hermann on increasing polarization driven by residential sorting. Documents election results showing Trede's victory defending her green government seat while Bichsel lost due to lack of urban support. Overall government remains in conservative hands with four bourgeois and three left seats.

stories.short_label: Wettertrend für Samstag und Sonntag
stories.summary: Samstag beginnt sonnig nach frostiger Nacht (-5°C). Von Westen ziehen Wolken auf, die zu einer Front gehören. Samstagabend und Nacht auf Sonntag: Front zieht durch mit verbreitetem Regen. Schneefallgrenze zwischen 400-800m im Westen. Montag Fortsetzung im gleichen Stil.

stories.short_label: Gestrandeter Buckelwal wird gerettet
stories.summary: An der deutschen Ostseeküste bei Lübeck versuchen Meeresexperten, einen gestrandeten jungen Buckelwal zu retten. Das über 10 Meter lange Tier sitzt seit drei Tagen auf einer Sandbank fest. Mit einem Bagger auf einem Floss graben Retter eine 50 Meter lange Rinne in die Sandbank, damit der Wal wegschwimmen kann. Erste Rettungsbemühungen mit einem kleineren Sandsaugbagger waren gescheitert.

stories.short_label: Verhaltenskodex for Signature Collection
stories.summary: Swiss Federal Chancellery introduces voluntary code of conduct to prevent signature fraud in popular initiatives and referendums. Code includes requirements for collector training, prohibition of payment based solely on quantity, and visible identification of commercial collection firms. Critics argue voluntary measures insufficient - no legal binding force or effective sanctions. Federal Chancellery reports suspected forgeries have declined significantly since 2024 due to enhanced controls.

stories.short_label: Trump Claims Iran Wants Deal Amid Military Escalation
stories.summary: Trump administration sends contradictory signals on Iran conflict in fourth week of war. Trump claims Iran desperately wants a deal while US simultaneously deploys thousands of Marines to region. Iran denies active negotiations, claims only indirect message exchange. Both sides list maximum demands: US wants Strait of Hormuz reopening, uranium enrichment halt, missile restrictions. Iran demands attack cessation and Hormuz control. US reports killing Iranian Revolutionary Guard Navy commander. Multiple Iranian attacks on Israel reported. No clear deescalation signals despite stated desire to end conflict.

stories.short_label: Cocaine Purity and Supply Surge in Switzerland
stories.summary: Swiss cocaine consumption doubled in past decade. Drug becoming increasingly pure (94-98% purity) and cheaper. Federal police intercept less than 10% of estimated several tons annually entering Switzerland. Supply routes via Antwerp port and Balkan/Italian distribution networks. Zurich dealer interview reveals high market purity standards unique to Switzerland. Expert Frank Zobel compares current cocaine flood to 1980s heroin epidemic. Federal criminal police call for decisive action. Documentary 'DOK' on SRF1 explores cocaine problem.

stories.short_label: Affordable Housing Shortage: Evicted Tenants in Asylum Facilities
stories.summary: Switzerland faces a severe affordable housing shortage. Municipalities struggle to house evicted tenants and homeless individuals. Investigation reveals one-third of Swiss cities and large municipalities accommodate homeless and evicted renters in asylum shelters alongside Ukrainian refugees. Cases documented in Rapperswil-Jona, Amriswil, Wädenswil, Dietikon, and Schwyz show increasing reliance on refugee facilities for Swiss citizens. Low-income residents compete with asylum seekers for limited emergency accommodation. Mixed housing creates tensions between vulnerable Swiss citizens and refugee populations.

stories.short_label: Impact on family, especially sister Dominique
stories.summary: Gisin discusses how her accident affected her family, particularly sister Dominique who believed her own injuries were meant to protect her siblings. She recounts Marc's severe accident and the family trauma it caused. Gisin explains how her cautious approach and close monitoring by Dominique made the accident feel like a breach of their agreement.

stories.short_label: Gisin family World Cup racing legacy
stories.summary: Discussion of the remarkable collective achievement of the Gisin family with approximately 550 World Cup races between them: Michelle with nearly 300, Marc with 100, and Dominique with around 150 races. The family achieved three Olympic gold medals collectively despite multiple severe injuries throughout their careers.

stories.short_label: Parents' emphasis on effort and commitment
stories.summary: Gisin discusses how her parents instilled values focused on effort and commitment rather than results. Her father would criticize lack of effort but never results. This philosophy, along with 'nothing should be too much for you,' became core to the family's approach to both skiing and life challenges.

stories.short_label: Recovery challenges and goal-setting
stories.summary: Gisin explains her rehabilitation approach, emphasizing the importance of goal-setting for mental motivation. She describes the difficult phase of rehabilitation where many things work well while others don't, and discusses how maintaining a goal prevents depression and keeps progress moving.

stories.short_label: Uncertain future regarding competitive skiing
stories.summary: Gisin discusses the decision about whether to return to competitive skiing, comparing her situation to Lara Gut-Behrami, Gino Caviezel, and Lindsey Vonn who faced similar decisions after injuries. She indicates she wants to test herself on skis in simple terrain before deciding whether to continue her career.

stories.short_label: Public judgment of athlete recovery decisions
stories.summary: Discussion of Lindsey Vonn's experience with public criticism after returning too quickly from injury. Gisin reflects on societal tendency to judge athletes' personal decisions, emphasizing the importance of respecting individual autonomy in determining what makes one happy.

stories.short_label: Vonn's recovery and public support
stories.summary: Gisin discusses Lindsey Vonn's recent severe accident at the Olympic Games and her uncertain recovery path. Gisin expresses respect for Vonn's comeback in training before the Olympics and reflects on the different support systems available in Switzerland versus abroad.

stories.short_label: Hintermann's decision to retire from skiing
stories.summary: Discussion of fellow skier Niels Hintermann's retirement decision after his cancer recovery. He chose to stop skiing rather than risk his second chance at life, expressing concern about the danger required for competitive skiing and preferring to retire on his own terms.

stories.short_label: Anxiety about returning to full performance
stories.summary: Gisin expresses understanding of the fear that one may never be able to push to the limit again after injury, particularly the concern about psychological barriers preventing return to full speed. She references panic attacks experienced by some skiers and the difficulty of balancing courage with safety.

stories.short_label: Fellow skier's decision to end career
stories.summary: Discussion of skier Joana Hählen who recently retired due to accumulated physical and mental toll of injuries. Gisin expresses admiration for Hählen's resilience and recovery efforts but respects her decision that mental reserves were exhausted despite successful return.

stories.canonical_keyword: Return to limit versus ability question
stories.short_label: Pushing limits and career continuation
stories.summary: Gisin articulates the sequence of decisions facing injured elite skiers: first whether they still want to ski, second whether they can do it, and third whether they can push limits. She emphasizes wanting to answer the 'do I want to' question while on skis rather than during physical therapy.

stories.short_label: Upcoming marriage to Luca De Aliprandini
stories.summary: Gisin discusses her upcoming wedding to Luca in June 2025, noting that the relationship has been strengthened through the difficult recovery period. She explains how having personal life focus during rehabilitation may provide beneficial distraction from skiing concerns.

stories.short_label: Post-wedding name question
stories.summary: Lighthearted exchange about potential name change after marriage, with Gisin joking that the combined name would be too long for television. Wrapping up discussion of personal future plans.

stories.short_label: Tourenskifahrer aus Gletscherspalte gerettet
stories.summary: In der Region Bernina hat die Rega gestern einen Tourenskifahrer aus einer Gletscherspalte gerettet. Eine Viererseilschaft war nahe des Gratkamms Bellavista auf 3'600 m unterwegs, als die Schneedecke unter einem Skifahrer einbrach. Dank rascher Reaktion der Begleiter blieb der Verunfallte am Seil hängen. Die Rega konnte ihn unverletzt retten.

stories.short_label: Iranian Regime Supporters in Switzerland
stories.summary: Investigative report into supporters of the Iranian Islamic Republic in Switzerland. Features Imam Seyyed Behbahani in Geneva leading a Shia Muslim community at Ahl-El-Bait foundation who defends the regime. Reveals connections between Swiss-based Islamic centers and Iranian state influence. Documents Mahmood Khalilzadeh, president of an Islamic center in Schlieren, who previously led a center in Frankfurt closed by German authorities for ties to Iranian extremism. Includes interviews with German interior minister on radical Islam and regime influence. Examines vulnerability of Swiss universities to regime influence through Iranian students and researchers.

stories.short_label: Winter bedroht blühende Obstbäume - Frostschutzmassnahmen
stories.summary: Der Winter kehrt zurück und gefährdet Obstbäume in Blüte mit Frostnächten bis -11°C. Obstbauern setzen Bewässerungssprinkler und Kerzen ein, um die Blüten mit Eismanteln zu schützen. Marco Frey vom Plantahof erklärt die Kristallisationswärme-Methode und warnt vor Temperaturen unter -7°C. Der Klimawandel führt dazu, dass die Blüte drei Wochen früher stattfindet und Schutzmaßnahmen bereits im März erforderlich sind.

stories.short_label: Municipalities House Evicted Tenants in Asylum Facilities
stories.summary: Investigation reveals Swiss municipalities are placing evicted tenants and homeless individuals in asylum facilities due to severe shortage of affordable rental housing. One-third of surveyed cities report housing homeless Swiss citizens in asylum or refugee facilities. Specific cases documented in Dietikon (26 homeless since 2015), Wädenswil (45 homeless, doubled in two years), Amriswil (10 men in former hotel alongside Ukrainian refugees), Rapperswil-Jona (3 displaced by building demolition), and Schwyz (nearly 100 persons including 2 Swiss in former hospital building with ongoing sanitation issues). Tensions arise when homeless Swiss and Ukrainian refugees share facilities. Swiss Constitution Article 12 mandates provision of shelter; financial responsibility clarified later.
